dtcs.description: Battery Voltage High SRSCM (Supplemental Restraints System Control Module) checks input voltage when "IG ON" to make air bag system work properly. If input voltage is out of normal range, there can be malfunction in system operation. In this case, Check battery and charging system. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1101 and turns warning light on if voltage above threshold value is detected for more than 4 sec. (If voltage within threshold value is detected When the SRSCM returns to normal condition. SRSCM regards DTC as being cleared and turns warning light off). DTC Detecting Condition Specification Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Current Data" mode.
Monitor the "Battery Voltage" parameter on the Scantool. Specification :Approx. Approx. 8 <= Vbatt <= 16 Fig.1) Normal Data
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Measure voltage between power terminal of the SRSCM harness connector and chassis ground. Specification :Approx. Approx. 8 <= Vbatt <= 16
Is the measured voltage within specifications? Ground Circuit Inspection

dtcs.description: Air Mix Potentiometer Open (Low)-Passenger The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) located at heater unit. It contains temp motor that changes temp door position and potentiometer that monitors position of temp door. The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) regulates the temperature by the procedure as follows. Signal from control unit adjusts position of temp door by operating temp motor and then temperature will be regulated by the hot/cold air ratio decided by position of temp door. In operation, potentiometer delivers temp door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU. Airconditioner Control Module sets DTC B1204 if the Feed Back signal of Passenger Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) has been detected open or below 0.1V for 0.1 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Air Mix potentiometer as a function of temp door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect diagnostic tool to the DLC.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start
Select "Air Mix Door Potentiometer-Passenger" parameter on the current data with scantool
Perform Actuation Test for Passenger Air Mix Door - 0% / 50% / 100%.
During the actuation test, verify the position sensor data. Specification :Check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer at current data should be close to the value of the acutation test.
Does the value of current data follow in accordance with the each actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Intake Potentiometer Open (Low) The intake actuator, mounted at the heater unit,controls the air intake source. The intake actuator consists of a motor and a feedback potentiometer. When driver operates the intake switch, ECU receives mode signal from intake switch and operates intake door motot to turn intake door to intended position. (with FRE mode signal, intake door is closed and with REC mode signal, intake door is opened) In operation, potentiometer delivers intake door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU. Airconditioner Control Module sets DTC B1208 if the Feed Back signal of Intake Actuator has been detected open or below 0.1V for 0.1 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Intake potentiometer as a function of position of Intake door Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start.
Select " Intake Potentiometer " parameter on the current data with scantool.
Perform Actuation Test for " Air Inlet Mode Selection - Reculation /Fresh in order.
With performing Actuation test, check that the value of each position sensors are changing. Specification :Recirculation : About 90%, Fresh : About 10%.
Are the value of each position sensors changed when performing actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: In-Car Temperature Sensor Short (Low) It contains a thermistor which measures the temperature of the inside. The signal,decided by the resistance value which changes in accordance with perceived inside temperature, is delivered to heater control unit. According to this signal, the control unit regulates incar temperature to intended value. Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B1233 if Incar temperature sensor has been detected below 0.1V for 4 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Resistance value of incar temp sensor as a function of temperature. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect diagnostic tool to the DLC.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature after engine starts.
Select and monitor "In-car temperature sensor" parameter. Fig.1) Parameter of "INCAR TEMP.SENSOR" will be fixed at 25°C(77°F), if there is any fault in INCAR SENSOR.
Is the Incar temperature sensor normal ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner
Is "voltage" display within specifications? Verification of Vehicle Repair Air Conditioner

The Ambient Temperature Sensor signal is used by the A/C ECU when making calculations related to blower motor, temperature, and mode door calculations.

dtcs.description: Evaporator Sensor Short (Low) The Evaporator sensor located on heater unit detects the core temperature. It is a negative type thermistor whose resistance is inversely proportional to temperature. Evaporator sensor transforms measured temperature into voltage value and delivers it to A/C ECU. when core temperature is blow threshold value, A/C ECU interrupts compressor relay power, in order to prevent evaporator freezing by excessive cooling. Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B1241 if Evaporator sensor has been detected below 0.1V for 4 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Resistance value of evaporator sensor as a function of temperature. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature after engine starts.
Select and monitor "Evaporator sensor" parameter on scantool. Fig.1) Parameter of "Evaporator Sensor" will be fixed at -2°C(28.4°F), if there is any fault in Evaporator Sensor.
Is the Evaporator Sensor normal ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner
Is "voltage" display near the specified value? Verification of Vehicle Repair Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: FIS(Front Impact Sensor)-Driver Defect Front Impact Sensor(FIS)located at both sides of the front of engine room detects head-on collision. When FIS delivers collision signal to SRSCM, SRSCM checks if safing sensor located in SRSCM detects collusion. and if both FIS and safing sensor detects collision simultaneously, SRSCM operates front air bag. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1328 if there is any fault in Driver side front impact sensor. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Diagnostic Trouble Codes(DTC)" mode.
Monitor diagnostic trouble code and present of trouble code.
Using a scan tool, clear the DTC.
Is DTC present problem ? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Is DTC present problem ? Verification of Vehicle Repair Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: Driver Airbag Resistance too High (1st stage) Driver Air bag module (hereinafter referred to DAB) located at center of steering wheel protects driver by reducing impact of collision. DAB is consist of air bag, pat cover and two inflator. There are power,circuit for ignition, gas generator and diffuser screen in inflator. Air bag reduces impact of collision by fillied up gas. In collision, pat cover splits and through this crack, air bag emerges and deploys. Inflator generates gas that expands air bag. Clock spring is located between steering wheel and column. It connects SCSRM to DAB. The SRSCM sets DTC B1346 if the measured resistance value of DAB circuit is more than the threshold value. *In this case, SRSCM checks if there's any fault in circuit by sending current for a while. DTC Detecting Condition Specification Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Current Data" mode.

Monitor the "Driver airbag(1st stage) resistance" parameter on the Scantool. Specification :1.5Ohms< Resistance of Driver airbag(1st stage) < 6Ohms Reference :In a case of an open in the Driver airbag(1st stage) circuit : FAIL In a case of a short to battery in the Driver airbag(1st stage) circuit: FAIL In a case of a short to ground in the Driver airbag(1st stage) circuit : FAIL Fig.1) Normal Data
Is parameter displayed within specifications? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Connect scantool to Data Link Connector(DLC) and clear DTC with scantool and diagnose again.
Is DTC present problem ? Clock Spring Circuit Inspection

dtcs.description: Firing Loops Interconnection Fault Every air bag module has its firing circuit that ignites powder to deploy air bag according to signal of SRSCM. SRSCM checks every air bag module when ignition "ON". Once any fault is detected, it is erased only by scantool. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1395 if there's any short circuit in harness of every firing circuit to one another. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Current Data" mode.

Monitor the "All airbag resistance" parameter on the Scantool. Specification :1.5Ohms< Resistance of all airbag < 6Ohms Reference :In a case of an open in the all airbag circuit : FAIL In a case of a short to battery in the all airbag circuit : FAIL In a case of a short to ground in the all airbag circuit : FAIL Fig.1) Normal Data
Is parameter displayed within specifications? Verification of Vehicle Repair Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: SIS(Side Impact Sensor) Front-Driver defect Side Impact Sensor(SIS) is located in both side of center pillar detects broad collision. When SIS delivers collision signal to SRSCM, SRSCM checks if safing sensor located in SRSCM detects collusion. and if both SIS and safing sensor detects collision simultaneously, SRSCM operates side air bag. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1400 if there is any fault in DSIS circuit. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select "Diagnostic Trouble Codes(DTC)" mode.
Monitor diagnostic trouble code and present of trouble code.
Using a scan tool, clear the DTC.
Is DTC present problem ? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: PODS(Passenger Occupant Detecting System) Sensor(Bladder) Defect The system is intended to classify the occupancy status of the front passenger seat in a motor vehicle based upon the measured force on the bottom seat cushion. The system also communicates to the SRSCM whether to allow or inhibit the deployment of the passenger air bags and/or pretensioner based upon this status. The System also measures dynamic responses of the occupant. This information is used to idenitify when a child seat is cinched down tightly with the seat belt, and to also determine if the seat is unoccupied. However, the dynamic measurements are not intended, nor capable of monitoring the seating position of the occupant, nor can they determine the proximity of the occupant to the inflator modules. The system should not be confused with an occupant position recognition system, or any other occupant proximitysensor. The Passenger Occupant Detecting System (PODS) utilizes bladder placed between the passenger seat cushionand suspension to measure the occupant's loading force on the vehicle seat. The bladder is connected to pressure sensor and ultimately to an electronic control unit(ECU), both of which are mounted under the seat pan. The quantitative force determined by the system is compared to a given threshold for determination of passenger airbag suppression. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1490 if there is any fault in PODS circuit. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

steps.instruction: Ignition "OFF", connect scantool.
Ignition "ON"& Engine "OFF", select Passenger Occupant Detecting System and select "PODS Reset" mode.
And then press "ENTER" Fig.1) PODS Rezero 1 Fig.2) PODS Rezero 2 Fig.3) PODS Rezero 3 Fig.4) PODS Rezero 4 Fig.5) PODS Real Time Status Of Current Data : Empty
Is DTC present problem ? W/Harness Inspection Airbag(Event #1)
Substitute the PODS ECU and check for proper operation.
Is DTC present problem ? Verification of Vehicle Repair Airbag(Event #1)

The system should not be confused with an occupant position recognition system, or any other occupant proximitysensor. The Passenger Occupant Detecting System (PODS) utilizes bladder placed between the passenger seat cushionand suspension to measure the occupant's loading force on the vehicle seat. The bladder is connected to pressure sensor and ultimately to an electronic control unit(ECU), both of which are mounted under the seat pan. The quantitative force determined by the system is compared to a given threshold for determination of passenger airbag suppression. DANGER :Do NOT strike or turnover the SRSCM with the "IG ON" as the seatbelt pretensioner, curtain airbag and/or side airbag could deploy. Handle the SRSCM with care. The SRSCM sets DTC B1493 if there is any error in communication between PODS Passenger Sensor and SRSCM. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Airbag(Event #1)

dtcs.description: Cluster Ionizer Fault The Cluster Ionizer makes a disinfection and decomposition of a bad smell from the air-conditioner or inflow air. And it cleans inside air of a vehicle.When running an air-conditioner or heater, it starts with "CLEAN MODE" on it and repeats from "CLEAN MODE" to "ION MODE" and from "ION MODE" to "CLEAN MODE" every 15 minutes. In the CLEAN MODE, the cluster ionizer generates negative ions and positive ions and makes a disinfection and decomposition of a bad smell from the air-conditioner or an inflow air. The cluster ionizer has functions as follows; a deodorization effect, a disinfection effect, and an air-cleaning effect. It has an operation indicator lamp on the upper side of its display area and shows whether it is operaing or not.In the ION MODE, the cluster ionizer generates negative ions and cleans inside air of a vehicle.It has an opeation indicator lamp on the lower side of its display area and shows whether if is operating or not. If the voltage of the cluster ionizer's diagnosis line is lower than 1.5V, the air-control module sets DTC B1688. DTC Detecting Condition Diagnostic Circuit Diagram W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Immobilizer TX Short Circuit To Ground This is DTC which is related with communication error between PDM and other units. ** Body CAN communication Control Units : BCM(Body Control Module), CLU(Cluster), PDM(Power Distribution Module), SMK(Smart Key), SJB(Smart Junction Box). * CAN (Controller Area Network) : CAN is serial bus communication type which links not only communication system but also control units each other. After insert the fob into the fob holder, press SSB button. Then, the communication state between the fob and the immobilizer is inputted to PDM. This code is outputted when the immobilizer data circuit is shorted to ground. (In this case, it is not possible to get authorization although the fob is in the holder.) dtcs.description: Air Mix Motor-Driver The temperature control actuator is located at the heater unit. The actuator consists of a motor and feedback potentiometer that is used by the A/C ECU to control air temperature and to monitor actuator operation. The Air mix actuator(Temperature actuator) regulates the temperature by the procedure as follows. Signal from control unit adjusts position of temp door by operating temp motor and then temperature will be regulated by the hot/cold air ratio decided by position of temp door. In operation, potentiometer delivers temp door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU. Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2406 if Driver air mix actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 10 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Air Mix potentiometer as a function of temp door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature afte engine starts.
Select and monitor "Air Mix Door Potentioner-Driver" parameter on scantool.
Select and perform Actuation test Air Mix Door Potentioner-Driver - 0% / 50% / 100% in order.

Check that the value of all the parameters are changed when performing the actuation test. Specification :Check that the value of Air Mix Door Potentiometer at current data should be close to the value of the acutation test.
Does the value of current data follow in accordance with the each actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Intake Motor The intake actuator, mounted at the heater unit,controls the air intake source. The intake actuator consists of a motor and a feedback potentiometer. When driver operates the intake switch, ECU receives mode signal from intake switch and operates intake door motot to turn intake door to intended position. (with FRE mode signal, intake door is closed and with REC mode signal, intake door is opened) In operation, potentiometer delivers intake door position transformed into voltage value to A/C ECU. Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2408 if Intake actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 10 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of Intake potentiometer as a function of position of Intake door Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal temperature after engine start.
Select " Intake Potentioner " parameter on the current data with scantool.
Perform Actuation Test for " Air Inlet Mode Selection - Reculation /Fresh in order.
With performing Actuation test, check that the value of each position sensors are changing. Specification :Recirculation : About 90%, Fresh : About 10%.
Are the value of each position sensors changed when performing actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner

dtcs.description: Direction Control Motor-Driver The mode control actuator is mounted at the heater control unit. The mode control actuator consists of a motor and a feedback potentiometer. Pressing mode select switch makes the mode control actuator shift in order of vent-> B/L -> floor -> mix. Air conditioner Control Module sets DTC B2409 if Driver Direction actuator has not been moved to the mode,where air condition control module controls, within 10 seconds. DTC Detecting Condition Specification ** Voltage value of potentiometer as a function of mode door position. Diagnostic Circuit Diagram Air Conditioner

steps.instruction: Connect scantool with diagnostic connector.
Warm up the engine to normal engine temperature afte engine starts.
Select and monitor "Direction Potention" parameter on scantool.
Select and perform Actuation test Driver Mode Door - Face / Foot / Defrost in order.
Check that the value of all the parameters are changed when performing the actuation test. Specification :Face - About below 10%, Foot : About 50%, Defrost : About 90%.
Are all the parameters changed when performing the actuation test ? W/Harness Inspection Air Conditioner
